   <item>
      <title>Blazers To Be 'Prime Trade Suitor' For Giannis Antetokounmpo, May Pursue Anthony Davis As Alternative</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285425/Blazers-To-Be-Prime-Trade-Suitor-For-Giannis-Antetokounmpo-May-Pursue-Anthony-Davis-As-Alternative</link>
      <description><p>The Portland Trail Blazers are set to emerge as a "prime trade suitor" for Giannis Antetokounmpo this offseason, according to Jake Fischer of The People's Insider.</p>
<p>The Blazers discussed deals for Antetokounmpo with the Bucks before the trade deadline.</p>
<p>The team is looking to build a trade package revolving around Jerami Grant's expiring contract. </p>
<p>The Blazers also own the Bucks' first-round picks in 2028, 2029, and 2030.</p>
<p>The Blazers also reportedly contacted the Mavs before the trade deadline over a possible deal for Anthony Davis. </p>
<p>They could still pursue Davis should they fall short on an Antetokounmpo deal. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Sat, 02 May 2026 22:52:12 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285425/Blazers-To-Be-Prime-Trade-Suitor-For-Giannis-Antetokounmpo-May-Pursue-Anthony-Davis-As-Alternative</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Antetokounmpo_Giannis_mil_251210.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Joel Embiid, Tyrese Maxey Lead 76ers Past Celtics In Game 7 To Complete Historic 3-1 Comeback</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285426/Joel-Embiid-Tyrese-Maxey-Lead-76ers-Past-Celtics-In-Game-7-To-Complete-Historic-3-1-Comeback</link>
      <description><p>Joel Embiid scored 34 points with 12 rebounds and six assists Saturday as the Philadelphia 76ers defeated the Boston Celtics 109-100 in Game 7, completing the first comeback from a 3-1 series deficit in franchise history. The Sixers advance to face the New York Knicks in the Eastern Conference semifinals.</p>
<p>Tyrese Maxey added 30 points, 11 rebounds and seven assists. Philadelphia held Boston to 40% shooting while the Celtics, playing without injured Jayson Tatum, made just two of their first 12 three-point attempts and never led after the second quarter.</p>
<p>"For him to do that for us and his team, man," Maxey said of Embiid. "Like, all we can do is appreciate him. He's been doing it all year, all his career really. And the city of Philadelphia should really not take it for granted and much love The Process."</p>
<p>Boston coach Joe Mazzulla started three players making their first career postseason appearances in Ron Harper Jr., Luka Garza and Baylor Scheierman. The Celtics fell behind 30-15 early but rallied to cut an 18-point fourth-quarter deficit to one before stalling. Boston missed three chances to take the lead in the final four minutes.</p>
<p>Tatum, who returned from a torn Achilles in March, was unavailable for the deciding game due to knee stiffness. Boston blew a 3-1 series lead against the seventh-seeded Sixers, raising significant questions heading into the offseason despite a strong regular season.</p>
<p>Philadelphia, healthy and cohesive, advances with growing confidence under team president Daryl Morey's roster construction around Embiid and Maxey.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">The Knicks are -260 favorites over the Sixers</a>, giving them a 72 percent implied probability of advancing to the Eastern Conference Finals. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Sat, 02 May 2026 23:20:26 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285426/Joel-Embiid-Tyrese-Maxey-Lead-76ers-Past-Celtics-In-Game-7-To-Complete-Historic-3-1-Comeback</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Embiid_Joel_phi_220208.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Kevin Durant Burner Question Was Significant Distraction Rockets Never Fully Resolved</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285418/Kevin-Durant-Burner-Question-Was-Significant-Distraction-Rockets-Never-Fully-Resolved</link>
      <description><p>Kevin Durant's first season with the Houston Rockets ended in disappointment, marked by a knee injury, a sprained right ankle, and unresolved questions about his relationship with teammates. The Rockets were eliminated by the Los Angeles Lakers in six games with Durant appearing in only of them and his future with the franchise uncertain less than one year after his trade from the Phoenix Suns.</p>
<p>Allegations surfaced during the season that Durant had criticized teammates, including Alperen Sengun and Jabari Smith Jr., through a burner account on X. Several league sources with close ties to Rockets players described the situation as a significant distraction that was never fully resolved internally.</p>
<p>"My teammates know what it is," Durant said during a media session in February. "We've been locked in the whole season."</p>
<p>Durant was one of just five NBA players to average 26 points, five rebounds and four assists during the regular season. However, the Rockets struggled with cohesion throughout the year.</p>
<p>A knee injury suffered in practice kept Durant out of Game 1 against the Lakers. He returned for Game 2 but sprained his right ankle in the second half, missing the rest of the series. Durant also drew attention in Game 3 by receiving treatment at Toyota Center rather than sitting with teammates on the bench during a pivotal collapse.</p>
<p>Ime Udoka defended the decision by Durant.</p>
<p>"He was back there getting treatment, getting the ice and doing things you can't do on the bench," Udoka said.</p>
<p>In October, <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/282421/Kevin-Durant-Rockets-Agree-To-Two-Year-$90M-Extension">Durant signed a two-year, $90 million extension that contains a player option for the 2027-28 season that will pay him $46 million</a>. Durant took $30 million less than his max available contract.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sat, 02 May 2026 09:15:04 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285418/Kevin-Durant-Burner-Question-Was-Significant-Distraction-Rockets-Never-Fully-Resolved</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Durant_Kevin_hou_251010.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Nikola Jokic Expected To Sign Four-Year, $278M Extension With Nuggets In Offseason</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285419/Nikola-Jokic-Expected-To-Sign-Four-Year-$278M-Extension-With-Nuggets-In-Offseason</link>
      <description><p>Nikola Jokic <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285397/Nikola-Jokic-Wants-To-Stay-With-Nuggets-Forever-Says-Team-Far-Away-From-Title">admitted a hard reality</a> that the Denver Nuggets are not currently close to title contention after they were eliminated in the first round of the playoffs, but he remains committed to the franchise and is expected to sign a contract extension this offseason. Jokic turned down a three-year, $212 million extension with the Nuggets last offseason, but can sign this summer for $278 million over four seasons.</p>
<p>While Jokic remains central to everything the Nuggets do, there could be significant changes to the roster around him.</p>
<p>"This is a team that has a lot of questions now going into the offseason," said Shams Charania on Stephen A. Smith's radio show on Friday. "I will tell you, the Denver Nuggets, from their top brass on down to that organization, they believe Nicole Jokic at his word. He wants to be there."</p>
<p>"This is not a situation where he's talking about potentially leaving, or looking elsewhere. Or saying 'Hey, I need you guys to do this. I need you guys to do that.'</p>
<p>"He's very, very internally and externally 'I'm here, no matter what, I'm re-signing.'</p>
<p>"And so that's the approach. But there are going to be changes in Denver. I don't know how much, but there's going to be some level of change. Whether that's tinkering a couple a couple players around... But they are so strapped financially moving forward in whether it's the tax, whether it's approaching the aprons, that there's going to be a lot of a lot of conversations about players on this roster."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sat, 02 May 2026 09:43:37 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285419/Nikola-Jokic-Expected-To-Sign-Four-Year-$278M-Extension-With-Nuggets-In-Offseason</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Jokic_Nikola_den_260507.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Anthony Edwards Tells Teammates He Plans To Return During Series Against Spurs</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285424/Anthony-Edwards-Tells-Teammates-He-Plans-To-Return-During-Series-Against-Spurs</link>
      <description><p>Anthony Edwards has informed his Minnesota Timberwolves teammates he intends to return at some point during their second-round series against the San Antonio Spurs, team sources told The Athletic. Edwards has been undergoing around-the-clock treatment on his injured left knee.</p>
<p>Chris Finch said Saturday that Edwards' week-to-week designation remains unchanged, making a Game 1 appearance on Monday all but impossible. Edwards did not practice Saturday but was on the court afterward getting shots up.</p>
<p>Edwards suffered a hyperextension and bone bruise in his left knee during Game 4 against Denver and did not play the final 2.5 games of Minnesota's first-round series. The Wolves advanced without him, winning two of those three games.</p>
<p>"Anytime you can get someone that's been hurt back, especially Ant, just the gravity that he carries, even him just being on the court will help us out a lot," forward Jaden McDaniels said. "Whenever he's ready, I told him we're going to hold it down until he gets ready."</p>
<p>Finch had signaled optimism about Edwards' availability before the Denver series ended, saying the Wolves would "be lucky enough to get him back" if they advanced. His right knee, which caused him to miss 11 of the final 14 regular season games, may also benefit from the additional rest.</p>
<p>Guard Ayo Dosunmu, who averaged 21.8 points against Denver, remains day to day with a sore right calf. Game 2 is Wednesday at San Antonio.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sat, 02 May 2026 22:42:11 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285424/Anthony-Edwards-Tells-Teammates-He-Plans-To-Return-During-Series-Against-Spurs</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Edwards_Anthony_min_260420.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Rockets Expected To Retain Ime Udoka, Rafael Stone; Will Discuss Long-Term Deal With Tari Eason</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285422/Rockets-Expected-To-Retain-Ime-Udoka-Rafael-Stone;-Will-Discuss-Long-Term-Deal-With-Tari-Eason</link>
      <description><p>Houston Rockets head coach Ime Udoka and general manager Rafael Stone are expected to return to the team next season, sources told Kelly Iko of Yahoo! Sports.</p>
<p>With back-to-back 50 win seasons, ownership remains confident in the duo. There is also a belief within ownership that with a healthy Steven Adams, Fred VanVleet, and Kevin Durant, the team is not far away from the Spurs or the Thunder.</p>
<p>The Rockets will be looking to make upgrades this summer. There was rumored regret for not making a move at the trade deadline for a guard or a big, and possible buyout additions remained with their current teams.</p>
<p>Among the names the Rockets are interested in this summer include Kawhi Leonard, Donovan Mitchell, Giannis Antetokounmpo and Joel Embiid.</p>
<p>The Rockets will also engage in long-term contract negotiations with Tari Eason and Amen Thompson this summer.</p>
<p>With Eason a restricted free agent, the Rockets are expected to match any offers.</p>
<p>Thompson will be eligible for a five-year, $252 million max deal. </p>
<p>The Rockets will also speak with VanVleet over a possible long-term deal this summer. VanVleet currently has a $25 million player option. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Sat, 02 May 2026 15:06:38 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285422/Rockets-Expected-To-Retain-Ime-Udoka-Rafael-Stone;-Will-Discuss-Long-Term-Deal-With-Tari-Eason</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Udoka_Ime_hou_231106.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Jayson Tatum Out For Game 7 With Knee Stiffness</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285421/Jayson-Tatum-Out-For-Game-7-With-Knee-Stiffness</link>
      <description><p>Jayson Tatum was ruled out of the Boston Celtics' Game 7 against the Philadelphia 76ers on Saturday night due to knee stiffness.</p>
<p>"He just came in today with knee discomfort," Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla said during his pregame media availability a few minutes after Tatum was ruled out. "And we decided, the medical staff and myself, that he would not play."</p>
<p>Tatum <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285402/Jayson-Tatum-Not-Concerned-About-Left-Calf-Tightness-Expects-To-Play-Game-7">left the Game 6</a> with left calf tightness.</p>
<p>"I expect to play," Tatum said following Game 6. "It was my other leg, not the one I injured last year. I mean, I wasn't like overly concerned."</p>
<p>Tatum is averaging 23.3 points and 10.7 rebounds in the first round. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Sat, 02 May 2026 13:48:49 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285421/Jayson-Tatum-Out-For-Game-7-With-Knee-Stiffness</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Tatum_Jayson_bos_260419.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Franz Wagner Out For Game 7 With Calf Injury</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285423/Franz-Wagner-Out-For-Game-7-With-Calf-Injury</link>
      <description><p>The Orlando Magic ruled Franz Wagner out for Game 7 against the Detroit Pistons due to a right calf strain.</p>
<p>Wagner will miss his third straight game since suffering the injury in Game 4.</p>
<p>With Wagner as the primary defender on Cade Cunningham, Cunningham scored a total of 17 points on 6-of-24 shooting.</p>
<p>Without Wagner, Cunningham is averaging 38.5 points per game while shooting 50% from the field. </p>
<p>Wagner averaged 16.8 points, 5.5 rebounds, 3.5 assists and 2.8 steals against the Pistons. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Sat, 02 May 2026 21:28:24 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285423/Franz-Wagner-Out-For-Game-7-With-Calf-Injury</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Wagner_Franz_orl_251104.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>LeBron James Embraces 'Small Victories' As Lakers Eliminate Rockets In Game 6</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285415/LeBron-James-Embraces-Small-Victories-As-Lakers-Eliminate-Rockets-In-Game-6</link>
      <description><p>LeBron James made NBA history during the Los Angeles Lakers' 98-78 Game 6 win over Houston Friday, becoming the oldest player to lead all participants in total points scored across a playoff series. James, at the age of 41, finished with 139 points over the six games</p>
<p>"It's not my style at all," James told ESPN after posting 28 points, eight assists and seven rebounds in the closeout victory. "I think me personally, the accepting and trying to be OK with the small victories comes with where I am in my career. Understanding, s---, who knows how many more playoff series I'm going to be a part of? If I play one more or two more or three more seasons, that doesn't guarantee me to be in the postseason. So just trying to appreciate the moments, whatever it is, and however long I'm playing. It's pretty cool to have the opportunity to lead a team, at 41 years old, into the playoffs and for us to be able to win it."</p>
<p>James entered the season in a reduced offensive role behind Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves. When both players were injured April 2 against Oklahoma City, he stepped back into the primary option and guided Los Angeles through the first round.</p>
<p>"I mean, to be able to experience something [new], it was just totally different for me," James told ESPN. "In March, it was a totally different role for me. It was super uncomfortable, but comfortable. I had to get into that. But it was also winning. That's all I care about. But I knew at the same time I could give more. But maybe giving more wasn't what was a fit for our team. And I accepted that and I was OK with that. Not from a team aspect, I was OK with that. I was able to put my ego to the side for the betterment of the team, understanding that I could do it."</p>
<p>Defensively, Los Angeles held Houston to 35% shooting. Deandre Ayton guarded Alperen Sengun individually after the Rockets exploited double-teams in Game 5, limiting Sengun to one assist in the closeout game.</p>
<p>"I took the challenge to my chin," Ayton said. "Just putting the team on my back and them trusting me. ... That's what really got everything going, to be honest."</p>
<p>Rui Hachimura added 21 points on 5-of-7 three-point shooting. Reaves contributed 15 points in just his second game back after missing four weeks with a left oblique injury. Los Angeles committed just 11 turnovers, their fewest of the series.</p>
<p>The Lakers now face defending champion Oklahoma City, which swept Los Angeles by an average of 29.3 points in four regular season meetings. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">Game 1 is scheduled for Tuesday with the Thunder favored by 14.5 points over the Lakers</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sat, 02 May 2026 07:58:04 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285415/LeBron-James-Embraces-Small-Victories-As-Lakers-Eliminate-Rockets-In-Game-6</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/James_LeBron_lal_260502.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Luka Doncic Has 'No Timeline' For Return, Still Hasn't Advanced To 1-On-1 Or Full Contact Work</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285420/Luka-Doncic-Has-No-Timeline-For-Return-Still-Hasnt-Advanced-To-1-On-1-Or-Full-Contact-Work</link>
      <description><p>Los Angeles Lakers guard Luka Doncic still has no timetable for his return from a Grade 2 hamstring strain, according to Cassidy Hubbarth of NBA On Prime.</p>
<p>"There's been no timeline, there has been some progress on the court. He's now doing some movement work in drills, getting up shots, but it is controlled," said Hubbarth on Friday night. </p>
<p>The Lakers, who <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285415/LeBron-James-Embraces-Small-Victories-As-Lakers-Eliminate-Rockets-In-Game-6">eliminated the Rockets in Game 6</a> of the first round, are now set to face the Thunder starting Tuesday. </p>
<p>Hubbarth added that Doncic still has not advanced to any contact work.</p>
<p>"He hasn't advanced to 1-on-1 or full contact work yet, and he's not into any scrimmages. So it's a little more than just spot shooting but still a step away from anything live ... this remains a slow build towards a return, not an imminent one," said Hubbarth. </p>
<p>It was reported last week that <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285357/Luka-Doncic-Unlikely-To-Play-In-Second-Round-If-Lakers-Advance">Doncic is unlikely to play</a> in the second round.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sat, 02 May 2026 13:09:41 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285420/Luka-Doncic-Has-No-Timeline-For-Return-Still-Hasnt-Advanced-To-1-On-1-Or-Full-Contact-Work</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Doncic_Luka_lal_260225.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Nuggets To Prioritize Retaining Peyton Watson</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285411/Nuggets-To-Prioritize-Retaining-Peyton-Watson</link>
      <description><p>The Denver Nuggets' top priority this offseason will be to retain Peyton Watson, sources told Jake Fischer of The People's Insider.</p>
<p>Watson is set to be an unrestricted free agent this summer. </p>
<p>It was reported that Watson will draw interest from the Bulls, Lakers, and Nets this offseason.</p>
<p>Executives around the league believe that <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285379/Peyton-Watson-Drawing-Interest-From-Lakers-Bulls-Nets-">Watson could receive a bigger contract</a> than Christian Braun's five-year, $125 million deal. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Sat, 02 May 2026 01:05:25 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285411/Nuggets-To-Prioritize-Retaining-Peyton-Watson</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Watson_Peyton_den_250112.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Hawks Unlikely To Make Big Trade This Offseason, Focused On Internal Improvements</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285427/Hawks-Unlikely-To-Make-Big-Trade-This-Offseason-Focused-On-Internal-Improvements</link>
      <description><p>The Atlanta Hawks are not looking to swing a big trade this offseason, according to Jake Fischer of The People's Insider. </p>
<p>The team is focused on making internal improvements, as well as deciding on the futures of CJ McCollum, Jonathan Kuminga, and Quin Snyder.</p>
<p>Hawks execs were encouraged by the team's improvement after trading Trae Young and Kristaps Porzingis at the deadline.</p>
<p>Executive Bryson Graham also remains a candidate to take over the Bulls head of basketball operations.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sat, 02 May 2026 23:24:49 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285427/Hawks-Unlikely-To-Make-Big-Trade-This-Offseason-Focused-On-Internal-Improvements</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_atlantahawks.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Thunder, Spurs Heavy Favorites In Western Conference Semis With Lakers, Wolves Facing Key Injuries</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285416/Thunder-Spurs-Heavy-Favorites-In-Western-Conference-Semis-With-Lakers-Wolves-Facing-Key-Injuries</link>
      <description><p>The Western Conference Semifinals are set, with the top-seeded Oklahoma City Thunder hosting No. 4 Los Angeles Los Angeles Lakers, and No. 2 San Antonio Spurs facing No. 6 Minnesota Timberwolves. Both series feature top-10 NBA players sidelined by injury, accentuating the favorite status of the Thunder and Spurs.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/team-stats/2026/Advanced_Stats/Team_Totals/Regular_Season">Oklahoma City and San Antonio finished first and second in Net Rating during the regular season</a>. Both are approximately <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">14-point favorites</a> in their respective Game 1 matchups.</p>
<p>Series odds reflect similar confidence. The Thunder open at -3000 and the Spurs at -2000, implying a 97% and 95% probability of advancing, respectively.</p>
<p>Luka Doncic, recovering from a Grade 2 left hamstring injury, is considered unlikely to return for the second round. Anthony Edwards is working back from a bone bruise and hyperextended knee suffered in Game 4 of the first round.</p>
<p>Minnesota advanced without Edwards, Donte DiVincenzo and Ayo Dosunmu in a Game 6 win over the Denver Nuggets. DiVincenzo is out for the season with a torn Achilles, leaving the Wolves to face Victor Wembanyama and San Antonio without their entire starting backcourt.</p>
<p>Oklahoma City swept Los Angeles 4-0 in the regular season by an average of 29.3 points, the largest point-differential gap between any second-round matchup this postseason.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sat, 02 May 2026 08:11:19 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285416/Thunder-Spurs-Heavy-Favorites-In-Western-Conference-Semis-With-Lakers-Wolves-Facing-Key-Injuries</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Gilgeousalexander_Shai_okc_260420.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>JJ Redick Says Lakers Winning Series Against Rockets 'A Big Deal'</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285414/JJ-Redick-Says-Lakers-Winning-Series-Against-Rockets-A-Big-Deal</link>
      <description><p>The Los Angeles Lakers defeated the Houston Rockets 98-78 in Game 6 to win the first-round series 4-2 on Friday night.</p>
<p>LeBron James finished with 28 points, with Rui Hachimura adding 21 points. Austin Reaves added 15 points in his second game back from an injured oblique. </p>
<p>James credited the Lakers' defense with the win.</p>
<p>"In order for us to win we had to protect the ball, we had to rebound and we had to be physical, make them take tough shots," James said. "I thought defensively we came in with a great game plan and we executed that thing to a T."</p>
<p>Amen Thompson lead the Rockets with 18 points, with Alperen Sengun adding 17 points.</p>
<p>Rockets head coach Ime Udoka expressed disappointment for overall series. It is the second straight year that the Rockets lost in the first round.</p>
<p>"Everybody's disappointed, no doubt," Udoka said. "Not what we expected coming into the game tonight or the series in general. We all thought that we'd be taking it back for a Game 7."</p>
<p>Lakers coach JJ Redick put his team's accomplishment without Luka Doncic into perspective.</p>
<p>"For us to be written off a few weeks ago and win a playoff series is a big deal," Redick said.</p>
<p>The Lakers will face the Thunder in the second round starting Tuesday.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sat, 02 May 2026 02:30:41 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285414/JJ-Redick-Says-Lakers-Winning-Series-Against-Rockets-A-Big-Deal</guid>

   <item>
      <title>Hollinger & Duncan: The 2026 Crucible Week 2, Lottery Reform</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285412/Hollinger-Duncan-The-2026-Crucible-Week-2-Lottery-Reform</link>
      <description><p><iframe title="YouTube video player" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/mPpsZNlNqOQ?si=ZNMk5Ravl_aXECam" width="560" height="315" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen="allowfullscreen"></iframe></p>
<div class="episode-long-desc" data-v-abc7255a="" data-v-17ef938d="">
<p>Hollinger and Duncan react to Minnesota's stunning series win over Denver, the Knicks' historic Game 6 destruction of Atlanta, the proposed lottery reform, and the biggest strategic developments of the playoffs so far. They also hit Celtics-Sixers, Pistons-Magic, Lakers-Rockets, and the players who have either survived or been exposed by the playoff crucible.</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Nuggets-Wolves fallout - 1:27:</strong> Minnesota's defense, Denver's offensive and defensive issues, the state of the Nuggets around Nikola Jokić, and whether Denver can still contend in a loaded West.</li>
<li><strong>Knicks-Hawks - 12:19:</strong> New York's historic beatdown of Atlanta, the Knicks' series adjustments, Karl-Anthony Towns and OG Anunoby's impact, and Atlanta's offseason questions.</li>
<li><strong>Lottery reform - 24:07:</strong> The proposed new system, its anti-tanking incentives, and why it could make late-season basketball more watchable.</li>
<li><strong>Best teams and contender check-in - 32:33:</strong> Oklahoma City vs. San Antonio, the most impressive playoff performances so far, and Boston-Philly heading into Game 7.</li>
<li><strong>Coaching and tactical takeaways - 36:58:</strong> The best and worst coaching ideas of the playoffs, including San Antonio's creativity and Portland's struggles against Victor Wembanyama.</li>
<li><strong>Pistons-Magic and East bracket chaos - 39:39:</strong> Orlando's depth issues without Franz Wagner, Detroit's concerning matchup problems, Cleveland's shaky path, and Toronto's impressive adjustments.</li>
<li><strong>The playoff crucible - 48:49:</strong> Which players have been exposed or survived under postseason pressure, including Atlanta's failed counters and Detroit's center questions.</li>
<li><strong>Lakers-Rockets and other series notes - 53:56:</strong> Houston's shortened rotation, Tari Eason's importance, the Lakers' health questions, Jalen Brown's off-arm struggles, and the second-round outlook.</li>
<li><strong>Final playoff thoughts - 58:14:</strong> Potential Knicks-Celtics, Sixers-Knicks, Thunder-Spurs, and Embiid-Harden scenarios, plus closing thoughts on the postseason landscape.</li>
</ul>
</div>
<div class="episode-long-desc" data-v-abc7255a="" data-v-17ef938d="">

   <item>
      <title>Duke To Play 3 Neutral-Site Games On Amazon Next Season</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285410/Duke-To-Play-3-Neutral-Site-Games-On-Amazon-Next-Season</link>
      <description><p>Duke have entered an enterprise partnership with Amazon that includes televising three games next season.</p>
<p>The package includes neutral-site games against UConn on November 25, Michigan on December 21, and Gonzaga on February 20. </p>
<p>The deal also includes NIL partnership opportunities to be announced as a later date.</p>
<p>"In addition to our outstanding partnership with ESPN, we are excited to work with Prime Video on this groundbreaking initiative," Duke athletic director Nina King said. "As Prime Video's first college sports partner, this collaboration not only expands the global reach of Duke Men's Basketball, but also creates meaningful opportunities for our student-athletes in a way that reflects innovation and excellence."</p>
<p>Players will be able to promote the games, presenting future revenue opportunities.</p>
<p> </p></description>
      <pubDate>Sat, 02 May 2026 00:43:14 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285410/Duke-To-Play-3-Neutral-Site-Games-On-Amazon-Next-Season</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Logo_Duke.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>RJ Barrett's Clutch Three-Pointer Forces Game 7 As Raptors Beat Cavs In Overtime</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285409/RJ-Barretts-Clutch-Three-Pointer-Forces-Game-7-As-Raptors-Beat-Cavs-In-Overtime</link>
      <description><p>RJ Barrett hit a three-pointer with 1.2 seconds remaining in overtime to lift the Toronto Raptors past the Cleveland Cavaliers 112-110 in Game 6 Friday, forcing a decisive Game 7 in Cleveland. The shot, which caromed high off the rim before dropping through, drew immediate comparisons to Kawhi Leonard's series-winning basket against the Philadelphia 76ers in 2019.</p>
<p>"I knew it was good because it went straight up. If it goes straight up, you've got a chance, like that [Tyrese] Haliburton shot," Barrett said.</p>
<p>"First thing I thought of was Kawhi Leonard, to be honest," Donovan Mitchell said, referencing Leonard's four-bounce buzzer-beater in 2019 when the Raptors defeated the Philadelphia 76ers in Game 7 of the Eastern Conference semifinals.</p>
<p>"It was only half a second, but it felt like an eternity," Raptors coach Darko Rajakovic said. "I was happy for him, for this team, for this city, that the shot went down."</p>
<p>Toronto played without starters Brandon Ingram and Immanuel Quickley, yet received standout contributions from its younger players. Scottie Barnes posted 25 points, 14 assists and seven rebounds. Barrett finished with 24 points and nine rebounds, Ja'Kobe Walter added 24, and rookie Collin Murray-Boyles contributed 17.</p>
<p>Cleveland held a late edge in regulation, leading by one with 18.8 seconds left after Jamal Shead split two free throws. The Raptors forced a critical turnover to regain possession, setting up Barrett's winning attempt.</p>
<p>"Gonna go to Cleveland, and Toronto, we'll be back real soon," Barnes told the crowd after the game.</p>
<p>Rajakovic had set the tone before tip-off.</p>
<p>"Grit. Who wants it more? Who's more desperate in the game tonight? ... Who's going to hit first? Who's going to go for a rebound, who's going to keep those 50-50 balls alive. We have to be that team tonight," Rajakovic said.</p>
<p>Cleveland's star duo of Mitchell and James Harden struggled throughout, combining for numerous turnovers and missed shots. Evan Mobley was the Cavaliers' standout performer, draining a three-pointer to tie the game with 11.6 seconds left in regulation and controlling the glass in overtime.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">Game 7 returns to Cleveland with the Cavaliers favored by 7.5 points over the Raptors</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 01 May 2026 23:26:21 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285409/RJ-Barretts-Clutch-Three-Pointer-Forces-Game-7-As-Raptors-Beat-Cavs-In-Overtime</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Barrett_RJ_tor_240122.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Pistons Overcome Historic 24-Point Deficit To Force Game 7 Against Magic</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285408/Pistons-Overcome-Historic-24-Point-Deficit-To-Force-Game-7-Against-Magic</link>
      <description><p>Cade Cunningham scored 32 points and the Detroit Pistons mounted one of the most remarkable comebacks in NBA playoff history Friday night, erasing a 24-point deficit to defeat the Orlando Magic 93-79 and force a Game 7 in their Eastern Conference first-round series. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">Detroit will host the deciding game Sunday and are favored by 9.5 points over the Magic</a>.</p>
<p>Orlando led 60-38 at halftime and pushed its advantage to 62-38 early in the third quarter. The Magic missed 23 consecutive field goal attempts, Detroit went on a 35-5 run, and Orlando finished the second half shooting 11% on 4-of-37 attempts, the worst field goal percentage by any team in any half during the play-by-play era dating to 1997-98, according to ESPN Research.</p>
<p>The Magic became the first team since 1996-97 to lose a home game after leading by at least 24 points with a chance to clinch a series.</p>
<p>"Detroit grit," Cunningham said. "That's what we've been talking about all year."</p>
<p>Orlando had outscored Detroit 35-12 in the second quarter, including a 17-0 advantage from three-point range and the free throw line.</p>
<p>"We took each possession at a time, both offensively and defensively, and tried our best to execute on every single possession," Pistons coach J.B. Bickerstaff said. "Every screening action, every rebound, all the small things. We went out and focused on that. And we put ourselves in position to win."</p>
<p>"When things get sideways, people splinter. And this group does the opposite," Duncan Robinson said. "It finds a way to just come together. There's a lot of pride in that locker room, not wanting to go out like that."</p>
<p>Tobias Harris added 22 points for Detroit. Paolo Banchero and Desmond Bane each scored 17 for Orlando, which is now 0-2 in closeout chances this series and remains without injured forward Franz Wagner.</p>
<p>"We've got to go do it the hard way," Magic coach Jamahl Mosley said.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 01 May 2026 23:22:03 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285408/Pistons-Overcome-Historic-24-Point-Deficit-To-Force-Game-7-Against-Magic</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Cunningham_Cade_det_260501.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Mark Cuban Admits He Tried To Buy Back Mavericks</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285407/Mark-Cuban-Admits-He-Tried-To-Buy-Back-Mavericks</link>
      <description><p>Mark Cuban confirmed he was involved in an effort to reacquire the Dallas Mavericks from the Adelson family, but acknowledged the attempt went nowhere after the new ownership group made clear the franchise is not available, Cuban told Front Office Sports in an interview set to air on an upcoming episode of Portfolio Players.</p>
<p>"If there was any chance of being able to do that anymore I would, but that's just not the game anymore," Cuban told FOS.</p>
<p>Cuban sold 73% of the team to the family of Miriam Adelson in December 2023 and has retained a 27% stake. However, the sale terms permit the Adelsons to purchase an additional 20% within four years, which would reduce Cuban's share to just 7%.</p>
<p>Earlier this year, a group of outside investors approached Cuban about partnering on a potential buyback. Cuban said he told them upfront that he doubted it would succeed.</p>
<p>"What happened was a bunch of people had contacted me; they weren't happy with the way things were," Cuban said. "And I was like, 'Look, if you can get them to sell, I would be more than happy to contribute my equity, et cetera, et cetera, and help.' But I didn't expect that to materialize. I told them I didn't think it would happen, that I didn't think the Adelsons had any interest in selling. And they don't."</p>
<p>The Adelson family responded to earlier reports of a potential buyback with a firm statement.</p>
<p>"The team is not for sale and the families look forward to expanding their ownership stake over time," the family said.</p>
<p>Cuban's frustration with the franchise's direction has been evident since the Adelsons traded Luka Doncic to the Los Angeles Lakers in February 2025. He previously stated on the Intersections podcast that he regretted not the sale itself, but the choice of buyer.</p>
<p>"I don't regret selling," Cuban said. "I regret who I sold to. I made a lot of mistakes in the process, and I'll leave it at that."</p>
<p>The 67-year-old did not rule out purchasing another NBA team, though he said geography and competitive bidding would present obstacles.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 01 May 2026 23:14:37 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285407/Mark-Cuban-Admits-He-Tried-To-Buy-Back-Mavericks</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Cuban_Mark_dal_180221.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>NBA Fines Mitchell Robinson $50,000, Dyson Daniels $25,000 Following Game 6 Ejections</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285406/NBA-Fines-Mitchell-Robinson-$50000-Dyson-Daniels-$25000-Following-Game-6-Ejections</link>
      <description><p>The NBA fined New York Knicks center Mitchell Robinson $50,000 and Atlanta Hawks guard Dyson Daniels $25,000 for their involvement in an on-court altercation during Game 6 of their first-round playoff series.</p>
<p>The incident occurred with 4:39 remaining in the second quarter of New York's 140-89 victory at State Farm Arena on April 30. Robinson and Daniels became entangled while competing for position during a free throw attempt, and the confrontation escalated after the two players had already been separated. Robinson continued to aggressively pursue Daniels following intervention by game officials. Both players were assessed technical fouls and ejected.</p>
<p>Robinson's larger fine reflects an additional factor beyond his on-court conduct. The league cited an inappropriate social media post he made referencing the altercation following the game.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 01 May 2026 22:51:09 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285406/NBA-Fines-Mitchell-Robinson-$50000-Dyson-Daniels-$25000-Following-Game-6-Ejections</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Robinson_Mitchell_nyk_190307.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Anthony Edwards Out To Start Series Against Spurs</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285405/Anthony-Edwards-Out-To-Start-Series-Against-Spurs</link>
      <description><p>Anthony Edwards will be out to start their second round series with a bone bruise and a left knee hyperextension. Edwards suffered the injury in Game 4 last Saturday and the injury typically requires a minimum two-week recovery.</p>
<p>The Minnesota Timberwolves advanced to the Western Conference Semifinals to play the San Antion Spurs in their Game 6 win over the Denver Nuggets despite playing without Edwards, Donte DiVincenzo and Ayo Dosunmu. </p>
<p>Edwards averaged 28.8 points, 5.0 rebounds and 3.7 assists in 35.0 minutes over 61 games during the regular season with the Wolves.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/teams/Minnesota-Timberwolves/17/playoff-history">The Wolves have reached the Western Conference Finals each of the past two seasons</a>, but they are <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">+950 underdogs</a> in their series against the Spurs.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 01 May 2026 14:05:14 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285405/Anthony-Edwards-Out-To-Start-Series-Against-Spurs</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Edwards_Anthony_min_251104.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>

   <item>
      <title>WNBA Franchise Values Rise 345 Percent Since 2024, Average Team Now Worth $427M</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285403/WNBA-Franchise-Values-Rise-345-Percent-Since-2024-Average-Team-Now-Worth-$427M</link>
      <description><p>The average WNBA franchise is now valued at $427 million, a 59% increase from 2025 and a 345% surge from 2024, according to Sportico. The collective value of the league's 13 teams that played in 2025 stand at $5.55 billion, including real estate and related assets.</p>
<p>The growth coincides with the league's 30th season, a newly ratified collective bargaining agreement, and lucrative broadcast deals taking effect in 2026. Those media agreements, shared with the NBA, allocate $2.2 billion to the WNBA over 11 years, representing a 500% increase over previous arrangements. Additional deals with Ion, CBS Sports, and USA Network push total media revenue to approximately $281 million annually.</p>
<p>The Golden State Valkyries lead all franchises at $850 million, followed by the New York Liberty at $600 million, the Indiana Fever at $560 million, and the Seattle Storm at $425 million.</p>
<p>The WNBA now carries the highest value-to-revenue multiple of any major sports league at 13.6, edging the NBA at 13.5, the NFL at 10.3, and the NWSL at 9.8.</p>
<p>The new CBA raises the salary cap from $1.5 million to $7 million in 2026. Despite the increase, multiple teams project profitability this year, supported by rising media and sponsorship revenue.</p>
<p>"I'm not sure I ever thought we'd be at the magnitude that we're at today," said Seattle Storm co-owner Lisa Brummel. "But it doesn't change anything about the way we think about our business. We still love being in the business we're in, and we run it the way we run it because we believe there is incredible upside."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 01 May 2026 11:05:41 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285403/WNBA-Franchise-Values-Rise-345-Percent-Since-2024-Average-Team-Now-Worth-$427M</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Wilson_Aja_lva_240522.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Jayson Tatum Not Concerned About Left Calf Tightness, Expects To Play Game 7</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285402/Jayson-Tatum-Not-Concerned-About-Left-Calf-Tightness-Expects-To-Play-Game-7</link>
      <description><p>Boston Celtics forward Jayson Tatum was spotted stretching out his left calf several times during his team's Game 6 loss at the Philadelphia 76ers. Tatum exited the contest for good in the third quarter and was later seen on the exercise bike. Despite the soreness in his left calf, Tatum expects to play in Game 7 on Saturday in Boston.</p>
<p>"I expect to play," Tatum said. "It was my other leg, not the one I injured last year. I mean, I wasn't like overly concerned."</p>
<p>The "other leg" Tatum is referencing is from when he tore his right Achilles' tendon in last season's playoffs. This injury is in the opposite leg, so the Celtics superstar doesn't feel like he'll miss any time.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 01 May 2026 09:24:27 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285402/Jayson-Tatum-Not-Concerned-About-Left-Calf-Tightness-Expects-To-Play-Game-7</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Tatum_Jayson_250503.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Timberwolves Unlikely To Allow Mavericks To Interview Tim Connelly</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285401/Timberwolves-Unlikely-To-Allow-Mavericks-To-Interview-Tim-Connelly</link>
      <description><p>The Dallas Mavericks had reportedly targeted Minnesota Timberwolves president of basketball operations Tim Connelly to fill their open lead front office role. However, in light of the team's success under Connelly, the Timberwolves are unlikely to grant the Mavericks permission to interview Connelly. Because he is under contract with the Wolves, they would need to allow the Mavs to interview Connelly for the position.</p>
<p>In Connelly's four seasons running Minnesota's front office, the Wolves have won nearly 60% of their regular season games. The Timberwolves have advanced to the last two Western Conference Finals and just completed an impressive upset of the Denver Nuggets in this year's first round of the playoffs.</p>
<p>Connelly was previously the GM of the Nuggets for nine seasons. He was a big part of building Denver's roster that won the NBA Finals in 2023, after he had left the team to run the Wolves basketball operations.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 01 May 2026 08:56:06 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285401/Timberwolves-Unlikely-To-Allow-Mavericks-To-Interview-Tim-Connelly</guid>

   <item>
      <title>Blazers Willing Make 'Big Splash' This Offseason If Price Is Right</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285400/Blazers-Willing-Make-Big-Splash-This-Offseason-If-Price-Is-Right</link>
      <description><p>Portland Trail Blazers GM Joe Cronin held his end-of-season media availability. One of the things that Cronin hit on was if the Blazers would be looking to make a major acquisition this summer. While Portland's GM didn't say his team definitely would make a big splash, he admitted the idea is enticing.</p>
<p>"Big splash is definitely something that's intriguing to us, but only at the right price point. We're not going to completely sacrifice our future for a short-term swing. But at the same time, we plan to be very active and aggressive and explore all opportunities out there," Cronin saind. "If none of those opportunities present themselves, we'll be content doing some smaller things, confident that our talent base is strong and that the addition of Dame (Lillard), the internal development, fine-tuning some of these skillset deficiencies we have, we can take a nice step whether we make a big splash or not."</p>
<p>Cronin said a key skillset Portland will be looking to add this offseason is shooting. He's hopeful that Lillard's return next season will help, along with having Vit Krejci for the full season, but Cronin know his team has to shoot better.</p>
<p>"I think, the Spurs series showed us that - really, the whole season - we just don't shoot the ball well enough," Cronin said.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 01 May 2026 08:45:19 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285400/Blazers-Willing-Make-Big-Splash-This-Offseason-If-Price-Is-Right</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Cronin_Joe_por_230615.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Jaden McDaniels, Wolves 'Prove Point' In Eliminating Nuggets In Game 6 Despite Injuries</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285399/Jaden-McDaniels-Wolves-Prove-Point-In-Eliminating-Nuggets-In-Game-6-Despite-Injuries</link>
      <description><p>Depleted by injuries to three starting guards, the Minnesota Timberwolves defeated the Denver Nuggets 110-98 in Game 6 on Thursday, advancing to the second round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs behind 32 points and 10 rebounds from Jaden McDaniels.</p>
<p>With Anthony Edwards, Donte DiVincenzo, and Ayo Dosunmu all sidelined, Minnesota adopted a frontcourt-heavy lineup. Rudy Gobert, Julius Randle, and Naz Reid powered a dominant 64-40 edge in points in the paint and a 50-33 rebounding advantage over Denver.</p>
<p>Terrence Shannon Jr., inserted into the starting lineup, contributed 24 points. His speed off the dribble proved difficult for Denver to contain, and a three-point play with 1:43 remaining extended Minnesota's lead to six.</p>
<p>McDaniels delivered the decisive blow, burying a 19-foot midrange pull-up to push the margin to seven with 1:06 to play. He then intercepted a pressured pass from Nikola Jokic to effectively seal the outcome.</p>
<p>"I'm just happy it's over, happy we were able to come out on top," McDaniels said. "Stuff was said. I'm just happy we were all able to prove our point, get the win, and move on to the next round."</p>
<p>Jokic posted 28 points, 10 assists and nine rebounds for Denver. However, Jamal Murray, closely guarded by McDaniels throughout, managed only 12 points on 4-for-17 shooting. Cameron Johnson led the Nuggets with 27 points, including 10-for-27 from three-point range as a team.</p>
<p>"This is what you don't really teach. This is about will. It's about heart, and that's how we were able to overcome this great challenge," said Gobert, who finished with 10 points, 13 rebounds and eight assists.</p>
<p>Minnesota, seeded sixth in the West, will face second-seeded San Antonio beginning Monday in the second round. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">The Spurs are 14.5 point favorites in Game 1</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 01 May 2026 07:56:54 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285399/Jaden-McDaniels-Wolves-Prove-Point-In-Eliminating-Nuggets-In-Game-6-Despite-Injuries</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/McDaniels_Jaden_min_231129.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Nikola Jokic Wants To Stay With Nuggets 'Forever', Says Team 'Far Away' From Title</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285397/Nikola-Jokic-Wants-To-Stay-With-Nuggets-Forever-Says-Team-Far-Away-From-Title</link>
      <description><p>Nikola Jokic said Thursday he still wants to finish his career with the Denver Nuggets, but the three-time MVP offered a candid assessment of where the franchise stands following a first-round playoff loss to the Minnesota Timberwolves.</p>
<p>"I still want to be a Nugget forever," Jokic said. "We just lost in the first round. I think we are far away."</p>
<p>The 31-year-old has two years and $121.9 million remaining on his current contract, with the second season a player option. Jokic becomes eligible this summer for an extension of up to four years and approximately $293 million.</p>
<p>Denver won 54 regular-season games and entered the playoffs as a realistic championship contender. Instead, the Nuggets fell 4-2 to a Minnesota team playing without Anthony Edwards and Anthony DiVinenzo for the final two games of the series.</p>
<p>Jokic shouldered significant responsibility for the defeat. He converted just 39 percent of his attempts in the first four games, a stark departure from his career 56 percent field goal rate. He acknowledged the shortcoming directly.</p>
<p>"A lot," Jokic said when asked how much blame he accepts. "I needed to play better. I must play better."</p>
<p>Co-star Jamal Murray struggled equally. Coming off a career-best 25.4-point regular season, Murray shot just 33 percent across the six-game series and missed 13 of 17 attempts in the decisive Game 6 against Jaden McDaniels.</p>
<p>Injuries further complicated Denver's situation. Wing Aaron Gordon missed three games with a calf injury, and Peyton Watson sat out the entire series with a hamstring strain.</p>
<p>Head coach David Adelman, completing his first full season after replacing fired Mike Malone, received public support from Jokic.</p>
<p>"It's not his fault we couldn't rebound," Jokic said. "There is nothing to blame David Adelman. It's all us."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 01 May 2026 06:37:38 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285397/Nikola-Jokic-Wants-To-Stay-With-Nuggets-Forever-Says-Team-Far-Away-From-Title</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Jokic_Nikola_den_240315.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Joel Embiid After Sixers Force Game 7 Versus Celtics: 'I'm Tired Of Losing To Them'</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285396/Joel-Embiid-After-Sixers-Force-Game-7-Versus-Celtics-Im-Tired-Of-Losing-To-Them</link>
      <description><p>The Philadelphia 76ers defeated the Boston Celtics 106-93 in Game 6 on Thursday night to force a Game 7 on Saturday.</p>
<p>Tyrese Maxey lead the scoring with 30 points, with Paul George adding 23 points. Joel Embiid finished with 19 points, 10 rebounds, and eight assists.</p>
<p>"I've been playing these guys for so long," Embiid said. "I'm tired of losing to them. So we have a chance to accomplish something special.</p>
<p>The Celtics have knocked Embiid out of the playoffs three times in his career.</p>
<p>Embiid pointed to his shot selection as the reason for his efficiency.</p>
<p>"You look at all the shots I'm getting, everything is short, in and out," Embiid said.</p>
<p>Jaylen Brown credited the Sixers defense with dictating the Celtics' offense.</p>
<p>"I think we're playing into their hands a little bit," Brown said. "I definitely think, even myself, the type of shots, the type of things they want us to do. That's not what we've been doing all year. But maybe a shift in your approach can open some stuff up offensively for us."</p>
<p>Jayson Tatum briefly left the floor in the third quarter with an apparent issue with his lower leg, though he downplayed the event after the game.</p>
<p>"Yeah, you all probably saw when I went to the back, saw me on the bike," Tatum said. "My leg just was a little stiff when I came out in the third quarter. But, you know, just kind of assessing the moment, like, the game was a little out of reach. </p>
<p>Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla added that Tatum was not hurt and will not be impacted for Game 7. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">The Celtics are 7.5 point favorites over the Sixers in Game 7</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 01 May 2026 02:18:58 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285396/Joel-Embiid-After-Sixers-Force-Game-7-Versus-Celtics-Im-Tired-Of-Losing-To-Them</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Embiid_Joel_phi_260406.jpg" />
   </item>
